decks.notes: The main idea of this Dimir Control deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan through a combination of countering spells, discarding their key cards with Thoughtseize and Quantum Riddler, and controlling the board with removal spells like Fatal Push. The deck also aims to generate card advantage with Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and utilize powerful creatures like Kaito, Bane of Nightmares and Murktide Regent to close out the game. With a good mix of disruption, card draw, and threats, this deck aims to control the tempo of the game and outvalue the opponent.

The deck revolves around quickly ramping up mana using rituals and mana generators, while also utilizing card draw and selection spells to find combo pieces. The key strategy is to generate a large amount of mana and cast Past in Flames to replay all instant and sorcery spells from the graveyard, allowing for a massive spell chain to finish the game. Ral, Monsoon Mage provides additional card draw and damage potential, while Wish acts as a versatile tutor for finding key combo pieces. The deck aims to overwhelm opponents with a powerful spell combo before they can respond.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through a combination of removal spells, powerful creatures, and card draw. By utilizing cards like Consign to Memory, Solitude, and Prismatic Ending, the deck can disrupt opponent's strategies while building up its own board presence with Phelia and Phlage. Cards like Quantum Riddler and Fable of the Mirror-Breaker provide card advantage and help maintain control over the game. Teferi, Time Raveler and Arena of Glory offer additional disruption and protection for the deck's game plan. Overall, the deck aims to outvalue and outlast opponents in a grindy matchup.

The deck revolves around a mix of aggressive creatures and disruptive spells to control the game. The focus is on utilizing cards that can generate value and provide tempo advantage, while also disrupting the opponent's game plan. Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phage, Titan of Fire's Fury serve as powerful threats that can quickly close out the game. Consign to Memory and Teferi, Time Raveler help to disrupt the opponent's strategy and buy time for the deck to establish dominance. Overall, the deck aims to apply pressure early on and maintain control throughout the game.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through counterspells, removal spells, and card draw. It utilizes a mix of disruptive cards like Teferi, Time Raveler and Orim's Chant to disrupt the opponent's game plan while maintaining card advantage with spells like Consult the Star Charts and Meticulous Archive. The deck also includes powerful finishers like Solitude and Wrath of the Skies to take control of the game once it's in a favorable position. Overall, the deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver the opponent to secure victory.

The key concept of this deck is to aggressively utilize cards that can easily deal damage to the opponent, such as Monastery Swiftspear, Dragon's Rage Channeler, and Lava Dart, while also manipulating card draw and selection with Expressive Iteration and Preordain. The deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with a combination of efficient creatures and direct damage spells, aiming to end the game swiftly before the opponent can establish their own game plan. The inclusion of Violent Urge and Slickshot Show-Off add additional pressure and potential for surprise bursts of damage.

This deck focuses on utilizing creatures with disruptive abilities to control the board and apply pressure on the opponent. By combining aggressive creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and powerful spells like Consign to Memory, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while quickly closing out the game. The inclusion of cards like Solitude and Phage, Titan of Fire's Fury adds resilience and additional threat potential to the strategy, making it a versatile and potent force in the Modern 1 vs 1 format.

The deck focuses on generating mana quickly using cards like Pyretic Ritual and Desperate Ritual, while also utilizing cards like Ral, Monsoon Mage and Glimpse the Impossible to draw cards and dig for combo pieces. The key goal is to assemble the necessary cards for a big combo turn, potentially utilizing Past in Flames to flashback key spells and eventually win the game with a massive Grapeshot or overwhelming the opponent with a high storm count. The deck is designed to quickly storm off and win the game in one explosive turn.

The deck focuses on using aggressive creatures and disruptive spells to control the opponent's resources and tempo. It utilizes powerful threats like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd, while also including disruptive cards like Prismatic Ending and Teferi, Time Raveler to disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck aims to apply pressure early and maintain control of the game through targeted removal and efficient creatures. Additionally, the deck includes cards like Consign to Memory and Arena of Glory to generate card advantage and maintain board presence.

This combo deck utilizes a toolbox strategy, enabling the player to search for and play specific creatures to disrupt the opponent's game plan while setting up their own win condition. The key concept revolves around utilizing creatures with various abilities and synergies, alongside cards like Agatha's Soul Cauldron and Malevolent Rumble to control the board and generate value. Yawgmoth, Thran Physician acts as a central piece in the deck, allowing for card draw, removal, and synergy with other creatures. The deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver the opponent through a combination of combo pieces and creature abilities.

This deck focuses on quickly deploying artifacts to maximize the power of cards like Krang, Master Mind and Weapons Manufacturing. The strategy is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive artifact creatures like Kappa Cannoneer and Pinnacle Emissary while controlling the board with cards like Engineered Explosives and Tormod's Crypt. Mishra's Bauble and Mox Opal provide additional card advantage and mana acceleration, allowing for explosive plays early in the game. With a mix of efficient threats and disruptive spells, this deck aims to close out the game before the opponent can stabilize.

The deck focuses on aggressively utilizing artifacts to quickly overwhelm the opponent and control the board. By combining efficient creatures like Kappa Cannoneer and Pinnacle Emissary with powerful artifact support like Mox Opal and Engineered Explosives, the deck can quickly establish a dominant board presence. Emry, Lurker of the Loch allows for recursion of key artifacts, while cards like Arcbound Ravager and Claws of Gix provide additional ways to manipulate the board state. The inclusion of disruptive spells like Metallic Rebuke and Tormod's Crypt help to disrupt the opponent's strategy while enabling the deck to maintain its aggressive pace.

The deck focuses on utilizing creatures with abilities that can be repeatedly triggered to create a powerful combo engine. By sacrificing creatures like Badgermole Cub or Young Wolf to Agatha's Soul Cauldron and Yawgmoth, Thran Physician, players can generate value and control the board. Boseiju, Who Endures provides protection for key spells, while Walking Ballista and Malevolent Rumble offer additional removal and win conditions. Green Sun's Zenith and Spymaster's Vault help tutor for key combo pieces, creating a versatile toolbox strategy that can adapt to different matchups.

The deck focuses on quickly casting cheap artifacts to take advantage of the affinity mechanic, allowing for explosive early game plays and overwhelming the opponent with a swarm of artifacts. By utilizing cards like Mox Opal and Urza's Saga, the deck aims to generate a large amount of mana and card advantage to maintain pressure on the opponent. The inclusion of control elements like Aether Spellbomb and Engineered Explosives helps to disrupt the opponent's game plan while the Aggro strategy relentlessly pushes for victory.

This deck emphasizes aggressive plays through a combination of creatures and spells that disrupt the opponent's game plan. It includes powerful creatures like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd alongside removal spells like Galvanic Discharge and Prismatic Ending. The deck aims to apply pressure quickly while also disrupting the opponent's strategies with cards like Teferi, Time Raveler and Casey Jones, Vigilante. It utilizes a mix of white, blue, and red mana sources to cast its spells efficiently and control the game tempo.

The deck focuses on disrupting opponents' game plans by using a combination of hand disruption, removal spells, and creatures that interact with the opponent's board. It aims to control the game through a mix of aggressive creatures like Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd and Overlord of the Balemurk, while also maximizing value through cards like Ephemerate and Teferi, Time Raveler. By applying pressure with efficient threats and disrupting the opponent's strategy, the deck aims to outpace and outvalue its opponents to secure victory.
